rag_rules: Place input assumptions in component type annexes when concrete bounds or visible relations are available
Use assign statements in implementation annexes only for existing output features or auxiliary variables
Avoid placing guarantees in implementation annexes; use component type annexes instead
Use exact Boolean expressions only when the port is an AGREE bool in scope; otherwise omit the clause
generic_rules: Use case-provided thresholds and legal visible feature names in AGREE contracts
Align helper declaration types with expression types to avoid type mismatches
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless explicitly exposed as ports or constants
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Use `eq` for equation declarations and `assign` for implementation-level bindings
Comparison expressions are bool; integer literals and arithmetic are int; real literals and arithmetic are real
anti_patterns: Avoid range comparisons or 0/1 equality over Boolean, enumeration-like, or opaque data ports
Do not place guarantees in implementation annexes
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
